Home / Revenue Projector

Revenue Projector

Project your MRR growth with quarterly milestones, compare growth scenarios, and see how long until your next revenue target.

$
$

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your current Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR).
2
Enter your average monthly growth rate as a percentage.
3
Select your projection period (6, 12, or 24 months).
4
Check the Revenue Forecast for your end MRR, total revenue, and annualized rate.
5
Review quarterly milestones to track your progress checkpoints.
6
Compare the three growth scenarios — conservative, current, and ambitious.
7
Read the Strategy Guide for stage-appropriate advice on what to focus on next.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good monthly growth rate for a SaaS?

For early-stage SaaS (under $10K MRR), 5-10% monthly is good. At $10K-$100K MRR, 3-5% is healthy. Above $100K MRR, 2-3% is normal as the law of large numbers kicks in. This calculator shows you projections at your current rate plus conservative and ambitious alternatives so you can plan for any scenario.

How do I increase my monthly growth rate?

Three levers: (1) More traffic — SEO, content marketing, paid ads, partnerships. (2) Better conversion — improve your landing page, offer a free trial, add social proof. (3) Less churn — nail onboarding, add a welcome email sequence, implement dunning (retry failed payments). A 1% improvement in each compounds to ~3% more monthly growth.

How accurate are long-term revenue projections?

The farther out, the less accurate. 6-month projections are fairly reliable if your growth rate is stable. 12-month projections are directional. 24-month projections are aspirational — use them for goal-setting, not budgeting. The scenarios section (conservative vs ambitious) helps you see the range of possible outcomes.

What if my MRR fluctuates month to month?

Use your 3-month average growth rate. If you grew 8%, then 3%, then 7%, your average is ~6%. Always plan with the conservative end of your range — it's better to be pleasantly surprised than to run out of cash. If your growth is highly variable, focus first on making it predictable.

When should I shift focus from growth to profitability?

When you have 6+ months of runway and predictable growth, start optimizing unit economics. If your growth is under 3%/mo and you have a solid customer base, profitability-first often beats growth-at-all-costs. This calculator helps you see the trade-off: a 5% growth rate gets you to $10K MRR much faster than 2%.

Results

Copy All
Example

📈 Revenue Forecast

📊 12-Month Projection • Starting MRR: $2,000/mo • Projected MRR: $3,592/mo • ARR Equivalent: $43,100/yr • Total Revenue: $30,397 over 12 months • Growth Multiple: 1.8× • Monthly Rate: 5.0% • Annualized Rate: 79.6% 🗺️ MRR Milestones • Q1 (Month 3): $2,315/mo • Q2 (Month 6): $2,680/mo • Q3 (Month 9): $3,103/mo • Q4 (Month 12): $3,592/mo ← target • To $10K MRR: 34 months (beyond current projection) 🔄 Growth Scenarios • Conservative (3.0%/mo) → $2,852/mo in 12 mo | $100K in 136 mo • Current Pace (5.0%/mo) → $3,592/mo in 12 mo | $100K in 81 mo • Ambitious (10.0%/mo) → $6,277/mo in 12 mo | $100K in 42 mo 🎯 Strategy Guide • Stage: Early traction — you have something people will pay for. • Focus: Double down on your top acquisition channel. Nail onboarding. • Pace: 🚀 Strong — compound interest is working hard for you. • $10K MRR is the first major milestone — on track in 34 months.
